title: Resume
text:
Yafei Zhang
    
      San Diego, CA  |  (445) 800-6477  | 
      yaz133@ucsd.edu  | 
      LinkedIn
    
    Download PDF
  

  
  Experience

  
    
      
        Score Framework & Customer Experience Analytics
        — Thermo Fisher Scientific Client
      
      Spring 2026 | In Progress
    
    
      Evaluating CX Score KPIs and weighting model performance, sensitivity, and relevance using descriptive, predictive, and prescriptive analytics to discover key drivers of customer experience outcomes and early change signals.
      Simulation and comparison of scoring and weighting schemes, resulting in data-driven recommendations and a CX framework for improved stakeholder trust, interpretability, and proactive customer experience management.
    
  

  
    
      
        Data Marketing Analyst
        — Levra Labs
        (Part-Time)
      
      Feb 2026 – Present
    
    
      Built an investor metrics workbook tracking MRR, ARR, LTV:CAC ratio, gross margin, and churn across 10 linked sheets — used by the CEO in every investor meeting.
      Researched and profiled 15+ partnership targets across three tiers, scoring each on revenue fit, team size, and strategic alignment to prioritize outreach.
      Created a competitive tracker comparing client company against two funded competitors ($78M and $62M raised), with gap analysis and investor rebuttal scripts.
      Developed a 5-year financial model projecting revenue across three streams (subscription, transaction, commission) with auto-updating growth assumptions.
    
  

  
    
      
        Data & Strategy Analyst
        — Hawaii DBET
        (Part-Time)
      
      May 2024 – Jul 2025
    
    
      Designed an automated, reproducible Python pipeline that analyzed 9 years of USDA Kona Coffee data (2015–2024) spanning production, yield, and pricing — replacing manual reporting, cut turnaround by 40%.
      Enabled stakeholders to stress-test seasonality, service level, and price sensitivity for export decision making.
      Identified the structural ceiling on coffee supply — 7,000 acres, yield variance of 3,150–4,180 lbs/acre, and a 62% price run-up ($1.55→$2.51/lb) over 9 seasons — for risk framework strategy making.
      Segmented Chinese B2B urban buyers and pressure-tested the margin resilience under disruptions, which shaped the premium positioning of export strategy's customer targeting.
    
  

  
    
      
        Marketing and Content Strategist
        — MirrorAI
        (Part-Time)
      
      Nov 2024 – Jul 2025
    
    
      Noticed friction in the report delivery flow and ran an A/B test that revealed 70% of customers preferred partial previews over a 3-minute wait — findings that drove a UX redesign and measurably improved user satisfaction.
      Spotted patterns in customer behavior data and partnered with Engineering and Product to weave those insights into the LLM development cycle, contributing to a 30% lift in customer retention.
    
  

  
  Project

  
    
      
        Global Wine Market Investment & Sourcing Strategy
        — Python (Polars), Tableau
      
      2025
    
    
      Consolidated 130K+ Kaggle wine evaluations, World Bank GDP, and worldwide trade production data into a clean analytical foundation utilizing Polars pipeline, regex vintage parsing, and manual country-name reconciliation.
      Developed a scoring model balancing quality ratings (60%) and production volume stability (40%) to distinguish premium-anchor markets (Italy, France, Spain) from high-volume growth plays (US, Australia, China).
    
  

  
  Specialized Skills

  
    
      Business Intelligence & Analytics:
      Agentic AI, Customer Segmentation, Customer Behavior Analysis, Funnel & Conversion Analysis, Operational Metrics Analysis, A/B Testing, Pricing & Sensitivity Analysis, Predictive Analytics
    
    
      Customer & Operations Analysis:
      KPI Design & Performance Tracking, Customer Lifecycle Analysis, Revenue & Profitability Analysis, Pipeline Performance Tracking, Process Optimization, Competitive Market Analysis
    
    
      Communication:
      Technical-to-Business Communication, Cross-Functional Communication, Slide Deck Development
    
    
      Data & BI Tools:
      SQL, Python, Tableau, Power BI, Excel (Advanced), Snowflake, Rhino3D, AI Assisted Workflow
    
  

  
  Education

  
    
      
        Master of Science in Business Analytics
        — Rady School of Management, UC San Diego
      
      Dec 2026
    
    Focus: Customer & Market Analytics, AI for Business, Applied Machine Learning | Coursework in uplift modeling and churn analysis
  

  
    
      
        Master of Arts in Media, Heritage, and Integrated Product Design
        — University of Pennsylvania
      
      May 2025
    
  

  
    
      
        Bachelor of Arts in Communication
        — University of Colorado Denver
      
      May 2023

title: Yafei Zhang
text:
As a data-driven business and marketing strategist with experience in business development and public relations, I am passionate about transforming data insights into impactful stories that boost companies’ revenue and enhance brand visibility. I am eager to use analytics skills like Python, SQL, and machine learning models to improve customer interactions, streamline operational processes, and optimize decision-making.

title: Global Wine Market Investment & Sourcing Strategy
text: Decision-makers needed a data-driven way to balance wine quality, pricing, production scale, and economic context when allocating sourcing and investment portfolios.

title: S-Mobile: Proactive Churn Management
text: S-Mobile faced a 2% monthly churn rate across 1 million subscribers — each percentage point representing millions in recurring revenue at risk. This project aimed to identify at-risk customers proactively and design cost-effective retention programs.

title: S-Mobile: Proactive Churn Management
text: S-Mobile faced a 2% monthly churn rate across 1 million subscribers — each percentage point representing millions in recurring revenue at risk. This project aimed to identify at-risk customers proactively and design cost-effective retention programs.

title: S-Mobile: Proactive Churn Management
text:
Modeling

Built a weighted Logistic Regression and Random Forest on a 27,300-customer training sample
Applied 50% oversampling with case weights (1:49) to calibrate predictions back to the real-world 2% churn rate
Achieved an AUC of 0.688 on the held-out test set

title: S-Mobile: Proactive Churn Management
text:
Key Insights
Used Permutation Importance and Partial Dependence Plots to identify the top actionable churn drivers:







Driver
Impact




Equipment age (eqpdays)
Customers with older devices churn significantly more


Overage minutes
High overage predicts dissatisfaction and churn


Refurbished handsets
Customers with refurbished devices have elevated churn risk



Together, these three variables affect customers representing over 30% of the subscriber base.

title: S-Mobile: Proactive Churn Management
text:
Retention Interventions
Designed 5 targeted retention actions using counterfactual simulation — modifying each variable in the model to predict what churn would look like if the action were taken:

Equipment upgrade program
Plan optimization for overage customers
Device replacement for refurbished handset holders
New customer loyalty incentives
Proactive outreach for high-risk segments

title: S-Mobile: Proactive Churn Management
text:
Economics
Evaluated 3 interventions using a 60-month CLV framework across 1M subscribers:

40% margin, 7.44% annual discount rate
Quantified both the indifference point and proposed intervention cost for each program
Determined net profitability to prioritize the highest-ROI retention actions

title: S-Mobile: Proactive Churn Management
text:
Skills Used
Random Forest · Logistic Regression · CLV Analysis · Python · Partial Dependence Plots · Counterfactual Simulation

title: Art & Heritage
text:
Where objects breatheand spaces remember
    
  

  
  
    
      
    
    
      Before I found my path in analytics, I fell in love with museums — the way
      a single artifact can collapse centuries into a moment of understanding.
      My graduate work at the University of Pennsylvania in
      Media, Heritage, and Integrated Product Design taught me to see
      design as a bridge between past and present, between the object and the visitor,
      between data and human experience.
    
  

  
  
    
      
        
      
      At the garden labyrinth — where landscape becomes language
    
    
      The Artist's Eye
      
        I believe the best design disappears. It doesn't announce itself —
        it makes you feel something before you understand why.
        A spiral hedge is not just topiary; it is a meditation on patience.
        A suspended sculpture is not decoration; it is gravity, reimagined.
      
      
        This is what I carry into every project: the instinct that beauty
        and rigor are not opposites — they are collaborators.
      
    
  

  
  
    
      The Reading Table
      
        Every practice begins with inquiry. These are the texts that shape
        how I think about cultural ownership, architectural language,
        and the politics of preservation.
      
    
    
      
        
      
      
        Restitution: The Return of Cultural Artifacts
        How to Read Modern Buildings
        The Cult of Creativity
      
    
  

  
  
    What Guides This Work
    
      
        01
        Cultural Narrative
        Every exhibition begins with a question: whose story is being told, and for whom? I design experiences that honor the complexity of heritage.
      
      
        02
        Material & Memory
        Objects are not static — they carry the weight of the hands that made them, the journeys they've traveled, and the meanings communities have given them.
      
      
        03
        Spatial Storytelling
        The gallery is a language. Light, distance, sequence, and silence — each element shapes how a visitor moves through understanding.
      
    
  

  
  
    
      
        Featured Collection
        Project Overview Portfolio
        
          A curated portfolio of heritage design work exploring museum exhibition
          concepts, cultural artifact interpretation, and integrated product design
          — developed during my graduate studies at UPenn's Weitzman School of Design.
        
        
          
            Institution
            University of Pennsylvania
          
          
            Program
            Media, Heritage & Integrated Product Design
          
          
            Year
            Spring 2024
          
        
        
          View Full Collection
          
        
      
    
  

  
  
    
      “
      
        This work shaped how I see data today — not as numbers on a screen,
        but as stories waiting for the right frame.
      
      
        Heritage taught me that context is everything.
        Analytics lets me prove it.

title: Through the Lens
text:
Where light meets water,I find my stillness.
    
  

  
  

  
    Photography is how I pause. Between data models and strategy decks, I chase golden hours along the California coast — drawn to the fleeting moments where light, water, and life converge. These are some of those moments.
  

  
  Golden Hour

  
    
      
      
        Homeward
        A silhouette at the edge of the world — the kind of moment that makes you stop mid-step.
      
    
    
      
      
        Still Warm
        Rocky shore, golden light, unhurried footsteps. The best company is the quiet kind.
      
    
    
      
      
        Liquid Gold
        Sunlight caught inside a wave — sometimes the most beautiful things last less than a second.
      
    
  

  
  Ocean & Sky

  
    
      
      
        Cotton Candy Skies
        That ten-minute window when the sky turns pastel and the ocean holds its breath.
      
    
    
      
      
        Evening Assembly
        Hundreds of birds lined up at the waterline, as if waiting for the last light to say goodnight.
      
    
    
      
      
        The Pier
        Huntington Beach at dusk — where surfers, seagulls, and strangers share the same golden hour.
      
    
  

  
  Wings & Waves

  
    
      
      
        Wingspan
        Caught mid-stretch — a small moment of wild grace at arm's length.
      
    
    
      
      
        Shared Shore
        We're all just visitors here — sandpipers, humans, and the tide.
      
    
    
      
      
        Quiet Company
        Two little ones, knee-deep in morning light. The beach belongs to the early birds.
      
    
  

  
  

  
    Every photograph is a small act of noticing — a reminder that beauty doesn't wait for us to be ready.
